ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER OF DEFENDANT TRANSBAY JOINT POWERS AUTHORITY TO PLAINTIFFS' SEVENTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT TRANSBAY JOINT POWERS AUTHORITY'S DEMURRER TO THE SEVENTH AND EIGHT CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR NUISANCE AND DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED AND THE COURT CONFIRMED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. HOWEVER, IF AND WHEN PLAINTIFFS SERVE TJPA WITH A TORTS CLAIMS ACT CLAIM COVERING NUISANCE AND DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY AND THE CLAIM IS DENIED OUTRIGHT OR BY OPERATION OF LAW BY INACTION, PLAINTIFFS MAY SEEK LEAVE TO AMEND TO ADD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NUISANCE AND DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY AGAINST TJPA. THE CLAIMS SERVED ON TJPA, WHICH THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF, WERE NOT DENIED BY TJPA WHEN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT WAS FILED AND DO NOT ADEQUATELY COVER CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NUISANCE AND DANGEROUS CONDITION OF PUBLIC PROPERTY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ronald Foreman of Foreman & Brasso telephone number 415-433-3475 for Plaintiff John Eng.
Andrew Schwartz of Shute, Mihaly & Weinberger LLP telephone number 415-552-7272 for Defendant Transbay Joint Powers Authority.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
Defendant Transbay Joint Powers Authority's demurrer to the seventh and eight causes of action in the first amended complaint for nuisance and dangerous condition of public property is sustained without leave to amend. However, if and when plaintiffs serve TJPA with a Torts Claims Act claim covering nuisance and dangerous condition of public property and the claim is denied outright or by operation of law by inaction, plaintiffs may seek leave to amend to add causes of action for nuisance and dangerous condition of public property against TJPA. The claims served on TJPA, which the court takes judicial notice of, were not denied by TJPA when the first amended complaint was filed and do not adequately cover causes of action for nuisance and dangerous condition of public property. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
